message.content:
Implement EXACTLY ONE task end-to-end in your isolated git worktree, run the gate, commit on the task branch, return the structured result JSON. Do NOT mutate the ledger; do NOT merge.

Task id: T97 (ledgerRefs goals:G4)
Branch: implement/T97    Base commit: a146fde (current main HEAD). If cut from a stale HEAD, `git reset --hard a146fde` first; confirm test/e2e harness (netns.go Topology, thresholds.go) + the T93 per-peer wiring + T94 per-peer /metrics labels exist before starting.
headline: Add netns e2e proving 2+ edges to one concentrator stay isolated
description: Add a netns e2e (test/e2e, appropriate build tag) with 2+ edges each bonded across its own uplinks to one concentrator: assert each edge's traffic resequences independently; one edge's loss/reorder/restart does not corrupt another edge's stream; return traffic routes to the correct edge; edge A NAT-rebind (source move) recovers via PROBE re-bind while B is unaffected; and a spoofed unbound-source flood degrades only bootstrap latency without cross-peer corruption or live-peer eviction (the threat model, end to end). Scrape the concentrator /metrics and assert per-peer-labeled series for both names. Follow the existing thresholds.go discipline; report-only where absolute numbers apply (M10/Q12).
acceptance: `go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e -run MultiPeer` passes on both e2e hosts (aarch64 + amd64): two edges' inner streams verify independently; killing+restarting edge A leaves edge B's tunnel uninterrupted; per-peer /metrics attribute traffic to the correct edge; the existing single-peer e2e tests still pass unchanged.

IMPORTANT: the -tags e2e netns suite needs privileged hardware you CANNOT run here — write the multi-peer e2e so it COMPILES and vets under -tags e2e (`go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e` + `go build -tags e2e ./test/e2e` must pass); note execution is DEFERRED to the o3.7mind.io + llm-ubuntu-0 hosts (G2 pattern). This is a substantial new fixture — a concentrator + 2+ edges each with its own uplinks. Read test/e2e/netns.go (Topology/SetupWithPaths), a multi-path sibling (e.g. failover_test.go, hub_failover_test.go), thresholds.go, and how /metrics is scraped (metrics.Fetch / PeerValue-PeerPathValue helpers T94 added) to build a multi-edge topology. Use a per-file-unique metrics port (survey existing *MetricsListen; the current max is ~9101).

Gate (run via `nix develop -c sh -c '<cmd>'`): `go build ./... && go vet ./... && go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e && go build -tags e2e ./test/e2e && go test ./...`. gofmt your changes.
If your worktree is not reachable for edits, `git switch -c t97-fix` off a146fde in your own worktree via the shared .git store, commit, report the EXACT sha. Return {taskId, status, resultCommit, branch, filesTouched, checkSummary, summary, blockedReason?} with the exact resultCommit sha.

content.content:
1	//go:build e2e
2	
3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"os/exec"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11		"testing"
12		"time"
13	)
14	
15	// pathSpec describes one emulated WAN uplink between the edge and concentrator
16	// namespaces: a veth pair carrying a /24, with netem delay+jitter on the edge
17	// egress. The bandwidth cap (rateMbit) and controlled loss (lossPct) are
18	// OPTIONAL config-time impairments: both default to zero, so the DefaultPaths
19	// topology stays uncapped and lossless and every existing P0/P1 e2e test runs
20	// unchanged. A non-zero rateMbit makes the LINK — not the single-core userspace
21	// WG crypto — the bottleneck ONLY when rateMbit sits below the EXECUTING host's
22	// measured in-fixture tunnel ceiling. That ceiling is CPU/PPS-bound (both daemons
23	// plus the load generator share the host's cores), a lower bound that scales with
24	// core count, NOT a link-throughput spec: ~12–46 Mbit/s single-flow on a 1-vCPU
25	// aarch64 host (docs/p0-findings.md:216-225), ~13 Mbit/s single-path (up to
26	// ~47–87 Mbit/s FEC single-flow) on a 4-vCPU amd64 host. Sizing rule: cap < ceiling
27	// for single-path, 2×cap < ceiling for aggregation. Below the ceiling
28	// a standing queue can form for bufferbloat/pacing (T21/T23) work; a non-zero
29	// lossPct injects uniform egress loss at Setup time for FEC-recovery (T25/T29)
30	// work.
31	type pathSpec struct {
32		name     string
33		edgeIP   string
34		concIP   string
35		edgeVeth string // <=15 chars
36		concVeth string
37		delayMs  int
38		jitterMs int
39		rateMbit int     // optional per-path bandwidth cap (netem rate); 0 = uncapped
40		lossPct  float64 // optional config-time uniform egress loss (netem loss); 0 = lossless
41	
42		// deferEdgeAddr, when true, makes Setup create and bring up the edge veth WITHOUT
43		// assigning edgeIP to it: the interface exists (so tc/netem still applies and the
44		// link is up) but the configured source_addr is not yet owned by any interface —
45		// the well-formed-but-not-yet-assignable condition T51's tolerant Open() defers and
46		// T55's background reconcile later promotes (T60). AddEdgeAddr adds the withheld
47		// address later. Zero value (false) is the default for every existing path, so
48		// Setup's behaviour for DefaultPaths and every other caller is unchanged.
49		deferEdgeAddr bool
50	}
51	
52	// DefaultPaths are the two emulated links: Starlink-like (low latency, jittery)
53	// and 5G-like (higher latency, stable).
54	var DefaultPaths = []pathSpec{
55		{name: "starlink", edgeIP: "10.100.1.1", concIP: "10.100.1.2", edgeVeth: "wbAe", concVeth: "wbAc", delayMs: 45, jitterMs: 10},
56		{name: "cellular", edgeIP: "10.100.2.1", concIP: "10.100.2.2", edgeVeth: "wbBe", concVeth: "wbBc", delayMs: 64, jitterMs: 0},
57	}
58	
59	// Topology is a two-namespace netns/netem fixture. The edge side is the current
60	// (re-exec'd) network namespace; the concentrator side is a child process's
61	// network namespace, addressed by PID so no writable /run/netns is required.
62	type Topology struct {
63		t      *testing.T
64		holder *exec.Cmd
65		pid    int
66		paths  []pathSpec
67	}
68	
69	// Setup builds the two-path topology from DefaultPaths (uncapped, lossless). It
70	// requires CAP_NET_ADMIN, which the e2e TestMain provides via an unprivileged
71	// user+net namespace (`unshare -Urmn`).
72	func Setup(t *testing.T) *Topology {
73		return SetupWithPaths(t, DefaultPaths)
74	}
75	
76	// SetupWithPaths builds the topology from an explicit path set, allowing a test
77	// to opt into the optional per-path bandwidth cap (rateMbit) and/or config-time
78	// loss (lossPct). DefaultPaths leaves both zero, so Setup is unchanged.
79	func SetupWithPaths(t *testing.T, paths []pathSpec) *Topology {
80		t.Helper()
81		top := &Topology{t: t, paths: paths}
82	
83		// Hold the concentrator network namespace open with a sleeping child.
84		top.holder = exec.Command("unshare", "-n", "sleep", "600")
85		if err := top.holder.Start(); err != nil {
86			t.Fatalf("start concentrator netns holder: %v", err)
87		}
88		top.pid = top.holder.Process.Pid
89		top.waitForNetns()
90	
91		pid := strconv.Itoa(top.pid)
92		top.run("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up")
93		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up")
94		// Pin net.ipv4.ip_nonlocal_bind=0 in the edge (test-process) network namespace, so
95		// a path whose source_addr is NOT assigned to any interface deterministically fails
96		// to bind (EADDRNOTAVAIL) instead of succeeding. That non-local-bind FAILURE is the
97		// premise the tolerant-startup tests (T60) rest on: a not-yet-assignable source_addr
98		// must DEFER (T51) and a config where NO path can bind must fail Open. A host that
99		// leaves the sysctl at 1 (or runs permissively) lets a non-local bind SUCCEED, which
100		// silently voids both premises (observed nondeterministic on a real host: a deferred
101		// path binding instead of deferring, or a zero-bindable daemon coming up). A fresh
102		// netns SHOULD default to 0, but pin it explicitly rather than trust the default.
103		// This affects ONLY non-local binds, so every other e2e test — which binds real,
104		// assigned addresses — is byte-for-byte unaffected. The concentrator netns needs no
105		// pin: every concentrator source_addr (concIP) IS assigned there (deferEdgeAddr only
106		// withholds the EDGE address), so the concentrator never binds a non-local address.
107		disableNonlocalBind(top.t)
108		for _, p := range top.paths {
109			// Idempotent pre-delete: the veth names are FIXED per path, so a prior subtest's
110			// teardown racing the kernel's async netns/veth reap can leave the edge veth behind,
111			// making `ip link add` fail with EEXIST ("File exists"). Deleting the pair first
112			// (ignore-if-absent) makes Setup robust to reused fixed names across sequential
113			// subtests. Deleting the edge end removes its peer too.
114			_ = top.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", p.edgeVeth)
115			top.run("ip", "link", "add", p.edgeVeth, "type", "veth", "peer", "name", p.concVeth)
116			top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.concVeth, "netns", pid)
117			if !p.deferEdgeAddr {
118				top.run("ip", "addr", "add", p.edgeIP+"/24", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
119			}
120			top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
121			top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", p.concIP+"/24", "dev", p.concVeth)
122			top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", p.concVeth, "up")
123			qargs := append([]string{"qdisc", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "root", "netem"}, top.netemArgs(p)...)
124			top.run("tc", qargs...)
125		}
126		t.Cleanup(top.Teardown)
127		return top
128	}
129	
130	// waitForNetns blocks until the holder's network namespace is observable.
131	func (top *Topology) waitForNetns() {
132		path := fmt.Sprintf("/proc/%d/ns/net", top.pid)
133		for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
134			if _, err := os.Stat(path); err == nil {
135				return
136			}
137			time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
138		}
139		top.t.Fatalf("concentrator netns %s never appeared", path)
140	}
141	
142	// netemArgs builds the netem parameter list for a path's baseline impairment
143	// profile: delay/jitter, then its configured loss (lossPct) and bandwidth cap
144	// (rateMbit) if set. For a DefaultPaths entry (both zero) this reduces to the
145	// prior delay/jitter-only output, so existing qdisc setup is byte-identical.
146	func (top *Topology) netemArgs(p pathSpec) []string {
147		return top.netemArgsWithLoss(p, p.lossPct)
148	}
149	
150	// netemArgsWithLoss is netemArgs with the loss percentage overridden — used by
151	// InjectLoss to set runtime loss while preserving delay/jitter and any rate cap.
152	// netem accepts the options in delay/loss/rate order.
153	func (top *Topology) netemArgsWithLoss(p pathSpec, lossPct float64) []string {
154		args := []string{"delay", fmt.Sprintf("%dms", p.delayMs)}
155		if p.jitterMs > 0 {
156			args = append(args, fmt.Sprintf("%dms", p.jitterMs))
157		}
158		if lossPct > 0 {
159			args = append(args, "loss", fmt.Sprintf("%g%%", lossPct))
160		}
161		if p.rateMbit > 0 {
162			args = append(args, "rate", fmt.Sprintf("%dmbit", p.rateMbit))
163		}
164		return args
165	}
166	
167	// path looks up a path by name, failing the test if absent.
168	func (top *Topology) path(name string) pathSpec {
169		for _, p := range top.paths {
170			if p.name == name {
171				return p
172			}
173		}
174		top.t.Fatalf("unknown path %q", name)
175		return pathSpec{}
176	}
177	
178	// RTT pings the concentrator end of the named path from the edge and returns the
179	// average round-trip time in milliseconds.
180	func (top *Topology) RTT(name string, count int) float64 {
181		p := top.path(name)
182		out := top.runOut("ping", "-c", strconv.Itoa(count), "-i", "0.2", "-W", "1", p.concIP)
183		// rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 37.264/48.520/82.043/19.354 ms
184		idx := strings.Index(out, "min/avg/max")
185		if idx < 0 {
186			top.t.Fatalf("path %q: no rtt line in ping output:\n%s", name, out)
187		}
188		eq := strings.Index(out[idx:], "=")
189		fields := strings.Fields(out[idx+eq+1:])
190		nums := strings.Split(fields[0], "/")
191		avg, err := strconv.ParseFloat(nums[1], 64)
192		if err != nil {
193			top.t.Fatalf("path %q: parse avg rtt %q: %v", name, nums[1], err)
194		}
195		return avg
196	}
197	
198	// Reachable reports whether the concentrator end of the named path answers pings.
199	func (top *Topology) Reachable(name string, count int) bool {
200		p := top.path(name)
201		return top.tryRun("ping", "-c", strconv.Itoa(count), "-i", "0.2", "-W", "1", p.concIP) == nil
202	}
203	
204	// InjectLoss sets uniform egress loss (percent) on the named path at runtime,
205	// preserving its delay/jitter profile and any configured bandwidth cap.
206	func (top *Topology) InjectLoss(name string, pct float64) {
207		p := top.path(name)
208		args := append([]string{"qdisc", "change", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "root", "netem"}, top.netemArgsWithLoss(p, pct)...)
209		top.run("tc", args...)
210	}
211	
212	// ClearLoss restores the named path to its configured baseline impairment
213	// (delay/jitter, plus any config-time loss and bandwidth cap). For a DefaultPaths
214	// entry that is delay/jitter only.
215	func (top *Topology) ClearLoss(name string) {
216		p := top.path(name)
217		args := append([]string{"qdisc", "change", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "root", "netem"}, top.netemArgs(p)...)
218		top.run("tc", args...)
219	}
220	
221	// Blackhole brings the named path's edge link down, simulating a WAN death (used
222	// by the P1 failover test).
223	func (top *Topology) Blackhole(name string) {
224		p := top.path(name)
225		top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "down")
226	}
227	
228	// Restore brings the named path's edge link back up.
229	func (top *Topology) Restore(name string) {
230		p := top.path(name)
231		top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
232	}
233	
234	// BlockEgress drops ALL outbound traffic on the named path's edge veth via a tc
235	// clsact egress filter (matchall + drop), while leaving the veth up and inbound
236	// traffic untouched — a one-way egress failure (e.g. a dead uplink amplifier), as
237	// opposed to Blackhole's bidirectional link-down. It is used by T104 to prove
238	// liveness is genuinely bidirectional: with only egress dead the peer's own probes
239	// still ARRIVE at this path (an operator naively trusting "receiving traffic" as
240	// health would stay wrongly up), but this path's OWN probe/echo round trip cannot
241	// complete, so it must go DOWN. clsact attaches its own ingress/egress hooks
242	// independent of whatever root qdisc is set, so it coexists with the path's netem
243	// root qdisc (delay/jitter/loss) without disturbing it.
244	func (top *Topology) BlockEgress(name string) {
245		p := top.path(name)
246		top.run("tc", "qdisc", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
247		top.run("tc", "filter", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "egress", "protocol", "all", "matchall", "action", "drop")
248	}
249	
250	// UnblockEgress removes a BlockEgress filter, restoring the named path's egress.
251	// Idempotent — deleting an already-absent clsact qdisc is tolerated — so a test may
252	// call it both explicitly and via t.Cleanup without failing on the second call.
253	func (top *Topology) UnblockEgress(name string) {
254		p := top.path(name)
255		_ = top.tryRun("tc", "qdisc", "del", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
256	}
257	
258	// Readdress replaces the edge-side address of the named path, simulating an edge
259	// public-IP change (used by the P1 roaming test). newEdgeIP must be a bare IPv4
260	// address in the path's /24.
261	func (top *Topology) Readdress(name, newEdgeIP string) {
262		p := top.path(name)
263		top.run("ip", "addr", "flush", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
264		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", newEdgeIP+"/24", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
265		top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
266	}
267	
268	// AddEdgeAddr adds the named path's configured edge-side address to its (already up)
269	// edge veth, WITHOUT flushing any existing address first (T60). It is the companion
270	// to a path built with deferEdgeAddr: that path's interface starts addressless, so
271	// this simulates the address becoming assignable after boot (e.g. a 5G modem's DHCP
272	// lease completing) for the T55 background reconciler to observe. Unlike Readdress —
273	// which flushes THEN re-adds, for the T16 re-roam case where an address is replaced —
274	// this only ADDS, because here there is nothing to flush.
275	func (top *Topology) AddEdgeAddr(name string) {
276		p := top.path(name)
277		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", p.edgeIP+"/24", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
278	}
279	
280	// nonlocalBindProc is the per-netns sysctl controlling whether a socket may bind a
281	// source address that is NOT assigned to any local interface. 0 (the value the
282	// tolerant-startup tests pin) makes such a bind fail EADDRNOTAVAIL; 1 lets it
283	// succeed. It is a plain proc file, so it is written/read directly rather than via
284	// the `sysctl` binary (one fewer external dependency).
285	const nonlocalBindProc = "/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_nonlocal_bind"
286	
287	// disableNonlocalBind pins ip_nonlocal_bind=0 in the CURRENT (edge/test) network
288	// namespace and asserts the value took, failing the test loudly if it cannot be set.
289	// A hard failure here — rather than proceeding — is deliberate: the tolerant-startup
290	// premise (a non-local bind must fail) is silently voided if the sysctl stays at 1,
291	// so an un-pinnable environment must surface as a test failure, never a false pass.
292	// os.WriteFile targets THIS process's netns, which is the edge/test namespace (the
293	// TestMain re-exec unshared it), exactly where the not-yet-assignable source_addr
294	// binds live. Writing a per-netns net sysctl needs the same privilege the fixture's
295	// veth/netem setup already requires, so anywhere the netns tier runs, this succeeds.
296	func disableNonlocalBind(t *testing.T) {
297		t.Helper()
298		if err := os.WriteFile(nonlocalBindProc, []byte("0\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
299			t.Fatalf("pin %s=0: %v (the tolerant-startup tests need a non-local bind to fail EADDRNOTAVAIL; without this a permissive host silently voids the premise)", nonlocalBindProc, err)
300		}
301		got, err := os.ReadFile(nonlocalBindProc)
302		if err != nil {
303			t.Fatalf("read back %s: %v", nonlocalBindProc, err)
304		}
305		if v := strings.TrimSpace(string(got)); v != "0" {
306			t.Fatalf("%s = %q after pin, want 0 — a non-local bind would still succeed and void the zero-bindable/deferred premise", nonlocalBindProc, v)
307		}
308	}
309	
310	// bringLoopbackUp brings the loopback interface UP in the CURRENT (edge/test)
311	// network namespace. It is a second, INDEPENDENT precondition for a non-local bind
312	// to fail EADDRNOTAVAIL: on this kernel the rejection fires only when at least one
313	// interface is UP — empirically, with lo DOWN a bind to a non-local address SUCCEEDS
314	// even at ip_nonlocal_bind=0 (kernel probe: lo-up+pin0 -> EADDRNOTAVAIL; lo-down+pin0
315	// -> BIND_OK; lo-up+pin1 -> BIND_OK). So the zero-bindable premise needs BOTH lo UP
316	// and the sysctl pinned to 0. SetupWithPaths already brings lo up for topology tests
317	// (line above), which is why the zero-bindable subtest passed only when it happened to
318	// run AFTER one; building no topology itself, it must bring lo up explicitly to hold
319	// regardless of subtest execution order. Idempotent (setting an already-up lo up is a
320	// no-op). Runs in this process's netns (the TestMain re-exec unshared it), like run().
321	func bringLoopbackUp(t *testing.T) {
322		t.Helper()
323		if out, err := exec.Command("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up").CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
324			t.Fatalf("bring lo up in the test netns: %v\n%s", err, out)
325		}
326	}
327	
328	// QdiscShow returns `tc qdisc show` for the named path (for assertions/debug).
329	func (top *Topology) QdiscShow(name string) string {
330		p := top.path(name)
331		return top.runOut("tc", "qdisc", "show", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
332	}
333	
334	// LinkExists reports whether the edge-side veth of the named path still exists.
335	func (top *Topology) LinkExists(name string) bool {
336		p := top.path(name)
337		return top.tryRun("ip", "link", "show", p.edgeVeth) == nil
338	}
339	
340	// Teardown removes the topology. Killing the holder drops the concentrator netns
341	// (and its veth ends); the edge-side veths are deleted explicitly. Idempotent.
342	func (top *Topology) Teardown() {
343		for _, p := range top.paths {
344			_ = top.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", p.edgeVeth)
345		}
346		if top.holder != nil && top.holder.Process != nil {
347			_ = top.holder.Process.Kill()
348			_, _ = top.holder.Process.Wait()
349			top.holder = nil
350		}
351	}
352	
353	func (top *Topology) run(name string, args ...string) {
354		top.t.Helper()
355		if out, err := exec.Command(name, args...).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
356			top.t.Fatalf("%s %s: %v\n%s", name, strings.Join(args, " "), err, out)
357		}
358	}
359	
360	func (top *Topology) tryRun(name string, args ...string) error {
361		return exec.Command(name, args...).Run()
362	}
363	
364	func (top *Topology) runOut(name string, args ...string) string {
365		top.t.Helper()
366		out, err := exec.Command(name, args...).CombinedOutput()
367		if err != nil {
368			top.t.Fatalf("%s %s: %v\n%s", name, strings.Join(args, " "), err, out)
369		}
370		return string(out)
371	}
372	
373	// iperfListenTimeout bounds waitIperfListen: how long to wait for the one-shot
374	// iperf3 server to reach the LISTEN state before failing the test.
375	const iperfListenTimeout = 5 * time.Second
376	
377	// waitIperfListen polls, inside the concentrator network namespace, for a TCP
378	// socket in the LISTEN state on the given port, and returns once it appears (or
379	// fails the test at iperfListenTimeout). It reads kernel socket state via
380	// `ss -ltn` and NEVER connects: the iperf3 servers here run one-shot (`-s -1`),
381	// so a probe-connect would consume the server's single accept and break the real
382	// client. It replaces the prior fixed sleeps at the iperf3Mbps and rttUnderLoad
383	// call sites, which raced a slow bind under load into 'connection refused' (D3).
384	func (top *Topology) waitIperfListen(t *testing.T, port int) {
385		t.Helper()
386		suffix := fmt.Sprintf(":%d", port)
387		deadline := time.Now().Add(iperfListenTimeout)
388		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
389			// ss -ltn columns: State Recv-Q Send-Q Local-Address:Port Peer-Address:Port.
390			out, err := exec.Command("nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "ss", "-ltn").CombinedOutput()
391			if err == nil {
392				for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
393					fields := strings.Fields(line)
394					if len(fields) >= 4 && strings.HasSuffix(fields[3], suffix) {
395						return
396					}
397				}
398			}
399			time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)
400		}
401		t.Fatalf("iperf3 server never reached LISTEN on port %d within %s", port, iperfListenTimeout)
402	}
403	
404	// nsenter runs a command inside the concentrator network namespace.
405	func (top *Topology) nsenter(args ...string) {
406		top.t.Helper()
407		full := append([]string{"-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n"}, args...)
408		top.run("nsenter", full...)
409	}
410

content.text: Now let me read the fixture and a multi-path test to understand how daemons are launched.

content.content:
1	//go:build e2e
2	
3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"fmt"
7		"os/exec"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11		"testing"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/telemetry"
15	)
16	
17	// T62 is the privileged netns e2e for the Q18 concentrator hub-failover feature
18	// (implemented in T57, internal/device/failover.go). It validates the edge-side
19	// active-standby switch END TO END: when EVERY path's liveness to the active
20	// concentrator (hub#1) goes DOWN simultaneously (HUB LOSS — the concentrator itself
21	// is gone, distinct from a single uplink dying), the edge advances to the next
22	// ordered endpoint (hub#2, a SEPARATE concentrator sharing the peer's SAME WireGuard
23	// static key), repoints the bond's remote, re-handshakes a FRESH session, and
24	// resumes carrying traffic through the standby — and the single-endpoint GUARD takes
25	// NO such action.
26	//
27	// TOPOLOGY (why a bridge). The two hubs must be (a) INDEPENDENTLY killable and (b)
28	// BOTH reachable from the edge's uplink, because the hub-failover switch repoints the
29	// WHOLE bond UNIFORMLY at ONE standby address (bind.Multipath.SetPeerRemote — invariant
30	// A1: the per-path fan-out beneath the single virtual endpoint is retargeted, the
31	// engine still sees one peer). A single edge uplink that can reach two distinct hub
32	// IPs models the REAL scenario — one WAN, the active concentrator dies, switch to a
33	// standby concentrator at a different public IP. In netns that shared medium is an L2
34	// bridge in the edge namespace with the edge and both hubs on one /24:
35	//
36	//	edge netns:            bridge wbHfbr = 10.100.0.1/24  (the edge uplink source_addr)
37	//	                        │
38	//	          ┌─────────────┴─────────────┐   (two bridge ports, two veth pairs)
39	//	   wbHfe1 │                     wbHfe2 │
40	//	   hub#1 netns:            hub#2 netns:
41	//	   wbHfc1 = 10.100.0.2/24   wbHfc2 = 10.100.0.3/24
42	//	   concentrator (hubKey)    concentrator (hubKey)   ← SAME WireGuard static key
43	//
44	// Both hubs run a concentrator daemon with the IDENTICAL WireGuard private key, so the
45	// edge's single peer (one public key) re-handshakes to EITHER — the standby is a fresh
46	// WG session to the same peer identity, NO hub-to-hub state handoff. Each hub is its
47	// own network namespace (a separate holder), so each owns its own `wanbond0` TUN
48	// (device.defaultTUNName is fixed, so two daemons cannot share one namespace) and is
49	// killed independently by bringing its bridge port down.
50	//
51	// ALL-PATHS-DOWN is driven by bringing hub#1's bridge port down (a REAL hub outage):
52	// the edge's probes to 10.100.0.2 are dropped at the bridge, so its prober(s) go
53	// StateDown together — exactly the liveness plane the controller keys on — while
54	// 10.100.0.3 (hub#2) stays reachable and the edge's own source (10.100.0.1 on the
55	// bridge) is untouched, so the re-handshake toward hub#2 can egress.
56	//
57	// The topology uses a SINGLE edge path deliberately: the controller's decision
58	// ("EVERY prober StateDown") and action (advance + SetPeerRemote + re-handshake) are
59	// identical for any path count, and after a uniform repoint the whole bond targets one
60	// hub socket regardless. The multi-path "ALL down, not just one" discrimination (a
61	// PARTIAL-down set takes no action) is already covered by T57's device-package unit
62	// test; this e2e proves the end-to-end switch on the real wire.
63	
64	const (
65		// Bridge + addresses for the two-hub failover fabric. All on one /24 so every
66		// node is L2-adjacent (no cross-subnet routing, no return routes). Veth names are
67		// <=15 chars (kernel IFNAMSIZ) and distinct from every other e2e file's veths.
68		hfBridge   = "wbHfbr"
69		hfEdgeIP   = "10.100.0.1"
70		hfHub1IP   = "10.100.0.2"
71		hfHub2IP   = "10.100.0.3"
72		hfHub1EP   = hfHub1IP + ":51820" // active concentrator endpoint (Endpoints[0])
73		hfHub2EP   = hfHub2IP + ":51820" // standby concentrator endpoint (Endpoints[1])
74		hfHub1Port = "wbHfe1"            // edge/bridge-side veth to hub#1 (brought DOWN to kill hub#1)
75		hfHub1Ceth = "wbHfc1"            // hub#1-side veth
76		hfHub2Port = "wbHfe2"            // edge/bridge-side veth to hub#2
77		hfHub2Ceth = "wbHfc2"            // hub#2-side veth
78		hfPathName = "uplink"            // the single edge path's name (the metrics series key)
79	
80		// hfMetricsListen is the edge /metrics endpoint for this file, on a port none of
81		// the other e2e files use (p2/p3/p4/pacing/tolerant use 9095-9098).
82		hfMetricsListen = "127.0.0.1:9099"
83		hfMetricsURL    = "http://" + hfMetricsListen + "/metrics"
84	)
85	
86	// hubFailoverSettleUpperBound mirrors the unexported device.hubFailoverSettle (3 s,
87	// docs/design.md §"Concentrator hub failover" → "Settle dwell"). It is the dwell a
88	// freshly-selected endpoint gets before another advance is allowed. It does NOT gate
89	// the FIRST switch in this test — the controller initialises lastSwitch to boot, and
90	// the tunnel bring-up + baseline-traffic phase before the kill already exceeds 3 s, so
91	// the boot dwell has elapsed by the time hub#1 is killed — but it is folded into the
92	// assertion window below as deterministic slack in case bring-up is unusually fast.
93	const hubFailoverSettleUpperBound = 3 * time.Second
94	
95	// hubFailoverWindow bounds how long the edge may take, after hub#1 is killed, to
96	// detect hub loss, switch to hub#2, re-handshake, and resume carrying traffic. It is
97	// an ANALYTICAL bound composed from the daemon's own timing constants (the D16
98	// single-source-of-truth style of thresholds.go), NOT a guessed sleep:
99	//
100	//	PLivenessFailoverBudget                     = DownAfter + 2*interval  = 1.6 s  (all-paths-DOWN detect + one interval headroom)
101	//	+ hubFailoverSettleUpperBound               =                          3.0 s  (settle dwell upper bound; see above — non-gating here)
102	//	+ DefaultUpSuccesses * DefaultProbeInterval  =                         0.6 s  (standby path UP-recovery: 3 echoes once probes reach hub#2)
103	//	+ 5 s                                        =                          5.0 s  (WG re-handshake RTT + CPU/PPS jitter on the shared netns fixture)
104	//	                                             ≈ 10.2 s
105	//
106	// The load generators here are ICMP/one-shot iperf3 (not a saturating flow), so the
107	// jitter term is generous rather than tight. The same window bounds the control test's
108	// NON-recovery poll, so the guard is given the FULL opportunity the positive path gets.
109	const hubFailoverWindow = PLivenessFailoverBudget +
110		hubFailoverSettleUpperBound +
111		time.Duration(telemetry.DefaultUpSuccesses)*telemetry.DefaultProbeInterval +
112		5*time.Second
113	
114	// TestHubFailoverStandbySwitch is the T62 positive acceptance: with an ORDERED
115	// two-endpoint list [hub#1, hub#2], the bond comes up on hub#1; killing hub#1 (a full
116	// hub outage, every path's liveness DOWN) makes the edge switch to hub#2, complete a
117	// FRESH re-handshake, and RESUME carrying traffic through hub#2 — within the analytical
118	// hub-failover window. The load-bearing proof is traffic-resumes-VIA-hub#2: with hub#1
119	// blackholed, any tunnel traffic can only traverse hub#2, and the post-switch iperf3
120	// server runs INSIDE hub#2's namespace, so a positive transfer is unambiguous. The
121	// edge's "hub failover:" WARN line (asserting the switch TARGET is hub#2's endpoint)
122	// corroborates the switch + re-handshake.
123	func TestHubFailoverStandbySwitch(t *testing.T) {
124		bin := buildWanbond(t)
125		// Ordered endpoint list: index 0 = hub#1 active, index 1 = hub#2 standby.
126		endpoints := fmt.Sprintf("[%q, %q]", hfHub1EP, hfHub2EP)
127		base, edge, h1, h2 := setupHubFailover(t, bin, endpoints)
128	
129		// (1) Bond up on hub#1: handshake + ping + a positive transfer through hub#1's
130		// namespace. waitPathUp confirms the single uplink's liveness is UP (=1).
131		if !base.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
132			t.Fatalf("bond never came up on active hub#1\n--- edge ---\n%s", edge.log())
133		}
134		waitPathUp(t, hfMetricsURL, hfPathName, 1, 15*time.Second)
135		if mbps := h1.iperf3Mbps(t, concInner, 3); mbps <= 0 {
136			t.Fatalf("active hub#1 carried non-positive throughput %.2f Mbit/s", mbps)
137		}
138	
139		// (2) Kill hub#1 entirely (bring its bridge port down): every path's liveness to
140		// the active concentrator goes DOWN together — HUB LOSS — while hub#2 stays
141		// reachable and the edge's own uplink source is untouched.
142		killAt := time.Now()
143		base.run("ip", "link", "set", hfHub1Port, "down")
144		t.Logf("killed active hub#1 (bridge port %s down) at T0", hfHub1Port)
145	
146		// (3) The edge must switch to hub#2 and RESUME traffic within the analytical
147		// window. With hub#1 dead, a successful ping to concInner can ONLY be carried by
148		// hub#2, so this alone proves the switch resumed traffic on the standby.
149		if !base.pingUntil(concInner, hubFailoverWindow) {
150			t.Fatalf("tunnel did not resume via standby hub#2 within %s of the hub#1 outage\n--- edge ---\n%s",
151				hubFailoverWindow, edge.log())
152		}
153		resumeGap := time.Since(killAt)
154	
155		// (4) The standby path's liveness recovered to UP (the bond re-established on
156		// hub#2), corroborating the ping.
157		waitPathUp(t, hfMetricsURL, hfPathName, 1, hubFailoverWindow)
158	
159		// (5) Traffic-resumes-VIA-hub#2, unambiguously: the iperf3 server runs INSIDE
160		// hub#2's namespace, so a positive transfer can only mean the WG session now
161		// terminates on hub#2 (hub#1 is still blackholed).
162		if mbps := h2.iperf3Mbps(t, concInner, 3); mbps <= 0 {
163			t.Fatalf("standby hub#2 carried non-positive throughput %.2f Mbit/s after failover", mbps)
164		}
165	
166		// (6) The switch + re-handshake is recorded in the edge journal, and it advanced
167		// to hub#2's endpoint specifically (the controller logs to_endpoint=next).
168		if !edgeLoggedHubFailover(edge.log(), hfHub2EP) {
169			t.Fatalf("edge journal has no 'hub failover:' switch to hub#2 endpoint %q; the controller must log the advance\n--- edge ---\n%s",
170				hfHub2EP, edge.log())
171		}
172	
173		t.Logf("HUB_FAILOVER_RESUME_MS=%d window_ms=%d: bond up on hub#1, hub#1 killed, edge switched to hub#2 (%s), re-handshaked, and resumed traffic via hub#2",
174			resumeGap.Milliseconds(), hubFailoverWindow.Milliseconds(), hfHub2EP)
175	}
176	
177	// TestHubFailoverSingleEndpointGuard is the T62 control / GUARD: with a ONE-element
178	// endpoint list [hub#1] the edge takes NO failover action when hub#1 dies — no switch,
179	// no re-handshake toward a standby, traffic does NOT resume — even though a healthy
180	// hub#2 is present and reachable in the fabric. This proves the switch is gated by the
181	// single-endpoint GUARD (len(endpoints) < 2), not merely by the absence of a standby:
182	// the standby EXISTS on the wire, it is simply not in the edge's endpoint list.
183	func TestHubFailoverSingleEndpointGuard(t *testing.T) {
184		bin := buildWanbond(t)
185		// ONE-element ordered list: only hub#1. hub#2 is still stood up and reachable, so
186		// non-recovery is attributable to the guard, not a missing standby.
187		endpoints := fmt.Sprintf("[%q]", hfHub1EP)
188		base, edge, h1, _ := setupHubFailover(t, bin, endpoints)
189	
190		if !base.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
191			t.Fatalf("bond never came up on hub#1\n--- edge ---\n%s", edge.log())
192		}
193		waitPathUp(t, hfMetricsURL, hfPathName, 1, 15*time.Second)
194		if mbps := h1.iperf3Mbps(t, concInner, 3); mbps <= 0 {
195			t.Fatalf("hub#1 carried non-positive throughput %.2f Mbit/s", mbps)
196		}
197	
198		// Kill hub#1. A single-endpoint deployment has no standby to advance to.
199		base.run("ip", "link", "set", hfHub1Port, "down")
200		t.Logf("killed hub#1 (bridge port %s down); single-endpoint list must NOT fail over", hfHub1Port)
201	
202		// The tunnel must STAY down for the full window the positive test uses to switch:
203		// a resumed ping here would mean a spurious failover to the (present, reachable)
204		// standby, violating the guard.
205		if base.pingUntil(concInner, hubFailoverWindow) {
206			t.Fatalf("tunnel resumed within %s under a SINGLE-endpoint list — a spurious hub failover; the guard (endpoints<2) must take no action\n--- edge ---\n%s",
207				hubFailoverWindow, edge.log())
208		}
209	
210		// And the controller must have logged NO switch.
211		if edgeLoggedHubFailover(edge.log(), "") {
212			t.Fatalf("edge journal contains a 'hub failover:' line under a single-endpoint list; the guard must emit none\n--- edge ---\n%s", edge.log())
213		}
214	
215		t.Logf("single-endpoint GUARD: hub#1 killed, no switch, tunnel stayed down for %s (a reachable hub#2 was NOT adopted) as required", hubFailoverWindow)
216	}
217	
218	// edgeLoggedHubFailover reports whether the edge journal contains a controller
219	// "hub failover:" switch line. When wantEndpoint is non-empty it additionally requires
220	// that line to name that endpoint (the controller logs to_endpoint=<next>), so the
221	// positive test asserts the switch TARGET; the control test passes "" to assert the
222	// mere ABSENCE of any such line.
223	func edgeLoggedHubFailover(logText, wantEndpoint string) bool {
224		for _, line := range strings.Split(logText, "\n") {
225			if !strings.Contains(line, "hub failover:") {
226				continue
227			}
228			if wantEndpoint == "" || strings.Contains(line, wantEndpoint) {
229				return true
230			}
231		}
232		return false
233	}
234	
235	// setupHubFailover builds the two-hub bridge fabric, starts both concentrator daemons
236	// (sharing ONE WireGuard static key) and the edge daemon (single path, the supplied
237	// ordered endpointsTOML), addresses all three TUNs, and returns the edge-namespace
238	// Topology handle (for ping/blackhole/run), the edge proc, and the two hub handles
239	// (for per-hub iperf3 into the correct namespace). endpointsTOML is the verbatim TOML
240	// array value for the edge peer's `endpoints` key, e.g. `["a:51820", "b:51820"]`.
241	func setupHubFailover(t *testing.T, bin, endpointsTOML string) (base *Topology, edge *proc, h1, h2 *Topology) {
242		t.Helper()
243	
244		base = &Topology{t: t}
245		base.run("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up")
246	
247		// Idempotent pre-delete: the bridge and edge-side veths have FIXED names in the
248		// shared edge namespace (the TestMain re-exec's netns is reused across this file's
249		// tests), so a prior test's teardown racing the kernel reap can leave them behind.
250		// Deleting first (ignore-if-absent) makes setup robust to sequential reuse.
251		_ = base.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", hfHub1Port)
252		_ = base.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", hfHub2Port)
253		_ = base.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", hfBridge)
254	
255		// The L2 fabric: an STP-off, zero-forward-delay bridge so ports forward the instant
256		// they come up (no listening/learning delay), carrying the edge uplink and both hubs.
257		base.run("ip", "link", "add", hfBridge, "type", "bridge", "stp_state", "0", "forward_delay", "0")
258		base.run("ip", "addr", "add", hfEdgeIP+"/24", "dev", hfBridge)
259		base.run("ip", "link", "set", hfBridge, "up")
260		t.Cleanup(func() {
261			_ = base.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", hfHub1Port)
262			_ = base.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", hfHub2Port)
263			_ = base.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", hfBridge)
264		})
265	
266		h1 = startHubHolder(t, base, hfHub1Port, hfHub1Ceth, hfHub1IP)
267		h2 = startHubHolder(t, base, hfHub2Port, hfHub2Ceth, hfHub2IP)
268	
269		// Shared identity: one WireGuard static key for BOTH hubs (the standby presents the
270		// SAME peer identity), one PSK for the outer control/probe plane.
271		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
272		hubPriv, hubPub := genKey(t)
273		psk := randKey(t)
274	
275		dir := t.TempDir()
276		// Edge: single uplink path (source_addr on the bridge), a metrics endpoint, and the
277		// ordered peer endpoint list. Level "info" so the controller's WARN "hub failover:"
278		// line is captured (it is suppressed at "error"). No per-path dest_addr: the peer's
279		// Endpoints[0] seeds the path remote (device.go:719), and the hub switch repoints it.
280		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
281	psk = "%s"
282	
283	[[paths]]
284	name = "%s"
285	source_addr = "%s"
286	
287	[metrics]
288	listen = "%s"
289	
290	[wireguard]
291	private_key = "%s"
292	
293	[[wireguard.peers]]
294	public_key = "%s"
295	endpoints = %s
296	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
297	
298	[log]
299	level = "info"
300	`, psk, hfPathName, hfEdgeIP, hfMetricsListen, edgePriv, hubPub, endpointsTOML, concInner))
301	
302		hub1Cfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "hub1.toml"), hubConfig(psk, hfHub1IP, hubPriv, edgePub))
303		hub2Cfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "hub2.toml"), hubConfig(psk, hfHub2IP, hubPriv, edgePub))
304	
305		// Concentrators first (they must be listening before the edge initiates), then edge.
306		h1.startProc(t, "hub1", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(h1.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", hub1Cfg)
307		h2.startProc(t, "hub2", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(h2.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", hub2Cfg)
308		edge = base.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfg)
309	
310		if !base.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
311			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
312		}
313		if !h1.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
314			t.Fatalf("hub#1 %s never appeared", tunDev)
315		}
316		if !h2.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
317			t.Fatalf("hub#2 %s never appeared", tunDev)
318		}
319	
320		// Address the edge TUN and BOTH hub TUNs. Both hubs carry the SAME inner address
321		// (concInner) in their OWN namespaces, so a ping to concInner is answered by
322		// whichever hub the WG session currently terminates on.
323		base.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
324		base.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
325		for _, h := range []*Topology{h1, h2} {
326			h.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
327			h.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
328		}
329		return base, edge, h1, h2
330	}
331	
332	// hubConfig renders a concentrator config on source_addr srcIP with the shared private
333	// key privB64 and the edge peer (pubB64). Log level "error": the hub's role in the
334	// assertions is to CARRY traffic (proven by iperf3 into its namespace), not to be
335	// log-scraped, so it stays quiet.
336	func hubConfig(psk, srcIP, privB64, edgePub string) string {
337		return fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
338	psk = "%s"
339	
340	[[paths]]
341	name = "%s"
342	source_addr = "%s"
343	
344	[wireguard]
345	private_key = "%s"
346	listen_port = %d
347	
348	[[wireguard.peers]]
349	public_key = "%s"
350	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
351	
352	[log]
353	level = "error"
354	`, psk, hfPathName, srcIP, privB64, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner)
355	}
356	
357	// startHubHolder opens a fresh network namespace (a sleeping child, PID-addressed like
358	// the base fixture's holder), wires a veth pair from the edge bridge into it, assigns
359	// hubIP, and brings lo + both ends up. It returns a Topology bound to the holder PID so
360	// the caller runs the daemon and iperf3 inside it. edgePortVeth is the bridge-side end
361	// (brought DOWN to kill the hub); hubCeth is the in-namespace end.
362	func startHubHolder(t *testing.T, base *Topology, edgePortVeth, hubCeth, hubIP string) *Topology {
363		t.Helper()
364		holder := exec.Command("unshare", "-n", "sleep", "600")
365		if err := holder.Start(); err != nil {
366			t.Fatalf("start hub netns holder: %v", err)
367		}
368		pid := holder.Process.Pid
369		t.Cleanup(func() {
370			_ = holder.Process.Kill()
371			_, _ = holder.Process.Wait()
372		})
373	
374		h := &Topology{t: t, pid: pid}
375		h.waitForNetns()
376	
377		// veth pair: bridge-side end onto the bridge, other end into the hub namespace.
378		base.run("ip", "link", "add", edgePortVeth, "type", "veth", "peer", "name", hubCeth)
379		base.run("ip", "link", "set", hubCeth, "netns", strconv.Itoa(pid))
380		base.run("ip", "link", "set", edgePortVeth, "master", hfBridge)
381		base.run("ip", "link", "set", edgePortVeth, "up")
382	
383		// `ip link set <dev> netns <pid>` is asynchronous w.r.t. the in-namespace address
384		// setup, and — critically — the device becoming visible to `ip link show` does NOT
385		// guarantee the immediately-following `ip addr add` succeeds: the netns move's attribute
386		// propagation lags visibility, so `addr add` can still fail "Cannot find device" (D33 —
387		// both observed hardware signatures: never-visible-in-time, and visible-but-not-yet-
388		// addressable). Retry the ACTUAL addressing operation (bounded), not a link-show proxy,
389		// so setup waits for the device to be genuinely usable in the target netns. On the first
390		// success we break, so `addr add` never runs twice (no duplicate-address error).
391		deadline := time.Now().Add(5 * time.Second)
392		for {
393			err := h.tryRun("nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(pid), "-n", "ip", "addr", "add", hubIP+"/24", "dev", hubCeth)
394			if err == nil {
395				break
396			}
397			if time.Now().After(deadline) {
398				t.Fatalf("could not add %s/24 to veth %q in hub netns %d within 5s after the netns move: %v", hubIP, hubCeth, pid, err)
399			}
400			time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
401		}
402	
403		h.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up")
404		h.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", hubCeth, "up")
405		return h
406	}
407

1	//go:build e2e
2	
3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"bytes"
7		"crypto/ecdh"
8		"crypto/rand"
9		"encoding/base64"
10		"encoding/json"
11		"fmt"
12		"os"
13		"os/exec"
14		"path/filepath"
15		"strconv"
16		"sync"
17		"syscall"
18		"testing"
19		"time"
20	)
21	
22	// P0 overlay addressing: the inner /24 carried by the WireGuard TUN on both ends.
23	const (
24		edgeInner  = "10.10.0.1"
25		concInner  = "10.10.0.2"
26		tunDev     = "wanbond0"
27		listenPort = 51820
28	)
29	
30	// TestP0PassThrough is the P0 acceptance gate: bring the wanbond tunnel up over a
31	// single path (no bonding) with the pass-through Bind, complete the WireGuard
32	// handshake, and verify both ICMP (ping) and a TCP bulk transfer (iperf3) flow
33	// through the tunnel between the edge and concentrator network namespaces.
34	func TestP0PassThrough(t *testing.T) {
35		top := Setup(t)
36		p := top.path("starlink")
37		bin := buildWanbond(t)
38	
39		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
40		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
41		psk := randKey(t) // outer-control PSK: required by config validation, unused by P0 pass-through
42	
43		dir := t.TempDir()
44		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
45	psk = "%s"
46	
47	[[paths]]
48	name = "starlink"
49	source_addr = "%s"
50	
51	[wireguard]
52	private_key = "%s"
53	
54	[[wireguard.peers]]
55	public_key = "%s"
56	endpoint = "%s:%d"
57	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
58	
59	[log]
60	level = "error"
61	`, psk, p.edgeIP, edgePriv, concPub, p.concIP, listenPort, concInner))
62	
63		concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
64	psk = "%s"
65	
66	[[paths]]
67	name = "starlink"
68	source_addr = "%s"
69	
70	[wireguard]
71	private_key = "%s"
72	listen_port = %d
73	
74	[[wireguard.peers]]
75	public_key = "%s"
76	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
77	
78	[log]
79	level = "error"
80	`, psk, p.concIP, concPriv, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner))
81	
82		// Concentrator first (it must be listening before the edge initiates), then edge.
83		conc := top.startProc(t, "concentrator", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg)
84		edge := top.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfg)
85	
86		// Wait for each side's TUN, then address it (wanbond owns the engine only;
87		// addressing is the operator's job — here, the test's).
88		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
89			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
90		}
91		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
92			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
93		}
94		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
95		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
96		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
97		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
98	
99		// The first ping triggers the handshake; retry until the session is established.
100		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
101			t.Fatalf("tunnel never came up: %s unreachable through the tunnel\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- concentrator ---\n%s",
102				concInner, edge.log(), conc.log())
103		}
104	
105		mbps := top.iperf3Mbps(t, concInner, 3)
106		if mbps <= 0 {
107			t.Fatalf("iperf3 through the tunnel measured non-positive throughput %.2f Mbit/s", mbps)
108		}
109		t.Logf("P0 tunnel up: handshake + ping OK, iperf3 = %.1f Mbit/s", mbps)
110	}
111	
112	// buildWanbond compiles the daemon (plain build, no e2e tag) into a temp path and
113	// returns it. The build uses the module-qualified package path so it resolves
114	// regardless of the test's working directory.
115	func buildWanbond(t *testing.T) string {
116		t.Helper()
117		bin := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "wanbond")
118		out, err := exec.Command("go", "build", "-o", bin, "github.com/7mind/wanbond/cmd/wanbond").CombinedOutput()
119		if err != nil {
120			t.Fatalf("build wanbond: %v\n%s", err, out)
121		}
122		return bin
123	}
124	
125	// genKey generates an X25519 keypair (the WireGuard key format) using the
126	// standard library, returning both keys base64-encoded as the TOML config
127	// carries them.
128	func genKey(t *testing.T) (privB64, pubB64 string) {
129		t.Helper()
130		priv, err := ecdh.X25519().GenerateKey(rand.Reader)
131		if err != nil {
132			t.Fatalf("generate X25519 key: %v", err)
133		}
134		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(priv.Bytes()),
135			base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(priv.PublicKey().Bytes())
136	}
137	
138	// randKey returns 32 random bytes base64-encoded (a placeholder PSK).
139	func randKey(t *testing.T) string {
140		t.Helper()
141		var b [32]byte
142		if _, err := rand.Read(b[:]); err != nil {
143			t.Fatalf("read random: %v", err)
144		}
145		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(b[:])
146	}
147	
148	// writeConfig writes body to path with the exact 0600 mode config.Load requires.
149	func writeConfig(t *testing.T, path, body string) string {
150		t.Helper()
151		if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(body), 0o600); err != nil {
152			t.Fatalf("write config %s: %v", path, err)
153		}
154		if err := os.Chmod(path, 0o600); err != nil { // defeat umask widening
155			t.Fatalf("chmod config %s: %v", path, err)
156		}
157		return path
158	}
159	
160	// lockedBuffer is a bytes.Buffer safe for concurrent Write (by exec.Cmd's output
161	// copier goroutine) and String (by the test's diagnostic log() calls).
162	type lockedBuffer struct {
163		mu  sync.Mutex
164		buf bytes.Buffer
165	}
166	
167	func (b *lockedBuffer) Write(p []byte) (int, error) {
168		b.mu.Lock()
169		defer b.mu.Unlock()
170		return b.buf.Write(p)
171	}
172	
173	func (b *lockedBuffer) String() string {
174		b.mu.Lock()
175		defer b.mu.Unlock()
176		return b.buf.String()
177	}
178	
179	// proc is a background process with captured combined output.
180	type proc struct {
181		cmd    *exec.Cmd
182		name   string
183		output *lockedBuffer
184	}
185	
186	func (p *proc) log() string { return p.output.String() }
187	
188	// startProc launches argv in the background, capturing its combined output and
189	// registering a cleanup that terminates it (SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after a grace
190	// period). A concentrator process is launched via nsenter so it runs — and
191	// creates its TUN — inside the peer network namespace.
192	func (top *Topology) startProc(t *testing.T, name string, argv ...string) *proc {
193		t.Helper()
194		cmd := exec.Command(argv[0], argv[1:]...)
195		out := &lockedBuffer{}
196		cmd.Stdout, cmd.Stderr = out, out
197		if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
198			t.Fatalf("start %s (%v): %v", name, argv, err)
199		}
200		p := &proc{cmd: cmd, name: name, output: out}
201		t.Cleanup(func() {
202			if cmd.Process == nil {
203				return
204			}
205			_ = cmd.Process.Signal(syscall.SIGTERM)
206			done := make(chan struct{})
207			// cmd.Wait (not Process.Wait) reaps the child AND waits for the output
208			// copier goroutines, so no write races the final log() read.
209			go func() { _ = cmd.Wait(); close(done) }()
210			select {
211			case <-done:
212			case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
213				_ = cmd.Process.Kill()
214				<-done
215			}
216		})
217		return p
218	}
219	
220	// waitLink polls until dev exists (in the peer netns when ns is true), up to d.
221	func (top *Topology) waitLink(dev string, ns bool, d time.Duration) bool {
222		deadline := time.Now().Add(d)
223		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
224			var err error
225			if ns {
226				err = top.tryRun("nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "ip", "link", "show", dev)
227			} else {
228				err = top.tryRun("ip", "link", "show", dev)
229			}
230			if err == nil {
231				return true
232			}
233			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
234		}
235		return false
236	}
237	
238	// pingUntil pings ip from the edge netns until it answers or d elapses. The first
239	// successful reply implies the WireGuard session is established.
240	func (top *Topology) pingUntil(ip string, d time.Duration) bool {
241		deadline := time.Now().Add(d)
242		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
243			if top.tryRun("ping", "-c", "1", "-W", "1", ip) == nil {
244				return true
245			}
246			time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)
247		}
248		return false
249	}
250	
251	// iperfDefaultPort is iperf3's default server port (5201), used by iperf3Mbps,
252	// which starts its server without an explicit -p. The under-load measurement uses
253	// the distinct bloatPort instead.
254	const iperfDefaultPort = 5201
255	
256	// iperf3Mbps runs a one-shot iperf3 TCP transfer to serverIP (inside the peer
257	// netns) for secs seconds and returns the sender throughput in Mbit/s.
258	func (top *Topology) iperf3Mbps(t *testing.T, serverIP string, secs int) float64 {
259		t.Helper()
260		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", serverIP)
261		top.waitIperfListen(t, iperfDefaultPort) // poll for LISTEN (never connect: server is one-shot)
262	
263		out := top.runOut("iperf3", "-c", serverIP, "-t", strconv.Itoa(secs), "-J")
264		var r struct {
265			End struct {
266				SumSent struct {
267					BitsPerSecond float64 `json:"bits_per_second"`
268				} `json:"sum_sent"`
269			} `json:"end"`
270		}
271		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &r); err != nil {
272			t.Fatalf("parse iperf3 json: %v\n%s", err, out)
273		}
274		return r.End.SumSent.BitsPerSecond / 1e6
275	}

3	package e2e

16	)
17	
18	// I2 is the netns e2e for the WG-session observability signal: the
19	// wanbond_session_established gauge, the wanbond_session_last_handshake_seconds age, and
20	// the ONE 'session established' INFO log record emitted on the 0->1 edge. Together they
21	// distinguish a tunnel that is STILL CONVERGING from one that is WEDGED — the signal
22	// D35/D36/D37 all presented identically without.
23	//
24	// EXECUTION IS DEFERRED (G2 pattern): the netns tier needs the privileged two-namespace
25	// fixture and is not run in the unit environment. This file must COMPILE and vet
26	// (`go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e` + `go build -tags e2e ./test/e2e`); the metric
27	// registration and the 0->1 edge are validated by the runnable unit tests in
28	// internal/metrics and internal/device.
29	const (
30		// i2MetricsListen is the edge's /metrics endpoint for this file's test. Each e2e file
31		// must use a port no other e2e file uses (collides under test shuffle/parallelism or a
32		// wedged teardown otherwise) — check the other *MetricsListen constants under test/e2e
33		// before picking a new one.
34		i2MetricsListen = "127.0.0.1:9101"
35		i2MetricsURL    = "http://" + i2MetricsListen + "/metrics"
36	
37		// i2SessionUpDeadline bounds how long the WG session may take to establish after the
38		// tunnel is up and carrying traffic. Generous: it must cover the edge's initial
39		// handshake plus the session monitor's next poll tick. It comfortably exceeds the edge
40		// keepalive so a keepalive-driven handshake also lands within it.
41		i2SessionUpDeadline = 30 * time.Second
42	)
43	
44	// TestSessionEstablishedTransitions is the I2 acceptance. It brings the bond up on a single
45	// path, drives inner traffic to force the WG handshake, and asserts:
46	//
47	//   - wanbond_session_established reaches 1 (scraped via metrics.Fetch) — the "->1" half of
48	//     the 0->1 transition. The "0->" half is asserted by the 'session established' log
49	//     record's existence: the device-side edge detector emits it ONLY on a false->true
50	//     transition, starting from false, so exactly one record proves a genuine 0->1 edge.
51	//     (Catching session=0 in an intermediate scrape is deliberately avoided — like the
52	//     path_up=1/session=0 window, the netns tier crosses it within ms and a scrape observer
53	//     would nondeterministically miss it.)
54	//   - wanbond_session_last_handshake_seconds is present and within the session-validity
55	//     window (a fresh handshake).
56	//   - the 'session established' record appears EXACTLY ONCE per session.
57	//   - the path-up-before-session-established ordering holds, by comparing the path-liveness
58	//     up transition and session-established transition TIMESTAMPS recorded in the logs (NOT
59	//     by observing an intermediate scrape): wanbond's own probe plane brings the path up
60	//     independent of the WG handshake, which only fires on inner traffic / keepalive, so the
61	//     path-up record must precede the session-established record.
62	func TestSessionEstablishedTransitions(t *testing.T) {
63		bin := buildWanbond(t)
64		path := DefaultPaths[0] // starlink
65		top := SetupWithPaths(t, []pathSpec{path})
66		edge, conc := setupI2Tunnel(t, top, bin, []pathSpec{path}, i2MetricsListen)
67	
68		// Force the WG handshake by driving inner traffic through the tunnel; without traffic the
69		// edge would only handshake on its 25s keepalive.
70		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
71			t.Fatalf("bond never came up on path %q\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", path.name, edge.log(), conc.log())
72		}
73	
74		// (1) wanbond_session_established reaches 1.
75		waitSessionEstablished(t, i2MetricsURL, i2SessionUpDeadline)
76	
77		// (2) The last-handshake age is present and fresh (within the validity window).
78		exp := scrapeMetrics(t, i2MetricsURL)
79		age, ok := exp.Value(metrics.MetricSessionLastHandshake)
80		if !ok {
81			t.Fatalf("%s series absent after the session established", metrics.MetricSessionLastHandshake)
82		}
83		if age < 0 || age > 180 {
84			t.Errorf("%s = %.1fs, want a fresh handshake within the 180s validity window", metrics.MetricSessionLastHandshake, age)
85		}
86	
87		// (3) The 'session established' record appears exactly once per session.
88		logText := edge.log()
89		if got := countLogRecords(logText, "session established"); got != 1 {
90			t.Fatalf("'session established' logged %d times, want exactly 1 per session\n%s", got, logText)
91		}
92	
93		// (4) path-up-before-session-established ordering, by log timestamps.
94		pathUpAt, ok := pathLivenessUpTime(logText)
95		if !ok {
96			t.Fatalf("no 'path liveness transition' to=up record in the edge log\n%s", logText)
97		}
98		sessionAt, ok := logRecordTime(logText, "session established")
99		if !ok {
100			t.Fatalf("no 'session established' record in the edge log\n%s", logText)
101		}
102		if sessionAt.Before(pathUpAt) {
103			t.Errorf("session established (%s) preceded path up (%s); expected path up FIRST — the probe plane brings the path up before the WG handshake fires on traffic/keepalive",
104				sessionAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), pathUpAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano))
105		}
106	
107		t.Logf("I2: path up at %s, session established at %s (once); last-handshake age %.1fs",
108			pathUpAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), sessionAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), age)
109	}
110	
111	// waitSessionEstablished polls url's /metrics until wanbond_session_established reads 1, or
112	// fails at deadline. A mid-poll scrape error is tolerated (transient), since the assertion
113	// is about the gauge VALUE reaching 1, not the endpoint's own availability.
114	func waitSessionEstablished(t *testing.T, url string, deadline time.Duration) {
115		t.Helper()
116		end := time.Now().Add(deadline)
117		for time.Now().Before(end) {
118			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 1*time.Second)
119			exp, err := metrics.Fetch(ctx, http.DefaultClient, url)
120			cancel()
121			if err == nil {
122				if v, ok := exp.Value(metrics.MetricSessionEstablished); ok && v == 1 {
123					return
124				}
125			}
126			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
127		}
128		t.Fatalf("wanbond_session_established never reached 1 within %s", deadline)
129	}

131	// i2LogRecord is the subset of a daemon slog JSON record this file asserts on: the emission
132	// time, the message, and the liveness transition's target state.
133	type i2LogRecord struct {
134		Time time.Time `json:"time"`
135		Msg  string    `json:"msg"`
136		To   string    `json:"to"`
137	}
138	
139	// logRecordTime returns the emission time of the FIRST log record whose msg equals the
140	// given message, and whether one was found.
141	func logRecordTime(logText, msg string) (time.Time, bool) {
142		for _, line := range strings.Split(logText, "\n") {
143			if !strings.Contains(line, msg) {
144				continue
145			}
146			var rec i2LogRecord
147			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &rec); err != nil {
148				continue
149			}
150			if rec.Msg == msg {
151				return rec.Time, true
152			}
153		}
154		return time.Time{}, false
155	}
156	
157	// pathLivenessUpTime returns the emission time of the FIRST 'path liveness transition'
158	// record whose target state is up, and whether one was found.
159	func pathLivenessUpTime(logText string) (time.Time, bool) {
160		for _, line := range strings.Split(logText, "\n") {
161			if !strings.Contains(line, "path liveness transition") {
162				continue
163			}
164			var rec i2LogRecord
165			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &rec); err != nil {
166				continue
167			}
168			if rec.Msg == "path liveness transition" && rec.To == "up" {
169				return rec.Time, true
170			}
171		}
172		return time.Time{}, false
173	}
174	
175	// countLogRecords counts the log records whose msg equals the given message.
176	func countLogRecords(logText, msg string) int {
177		n := 0
178		for _, line := range strings.Split(logText, "\n") {
179			if !strings.Contains(line, msg) {
180				continue
181			}
182			var rec i2LogRecord
183			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &rec); err != nil {
184				continue
185			}
186			if rec.Msg == msg {
187				n++
188			}
189		}
190		return n
191	}
192	
193	// setupI2Tunnel mirrors setupT60Tunnel's config shape and bring-up sequence, adding an edge
194	// [metrics] listen block AND running the edge at [log] level "info": I2 asserts on the INFO
195	// 'session established' and 'path liveness transition' records, which an "error" level would
196	// suppress.
197	func setupI2Tunnel(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, paths []pathSpec, metricsListen string) (edge, conc *proc) {
198		t.Helper()
199	
200		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
201		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
202		psk := randKey(t)
203	
204		var edgePaths, concPaths strings.Builder
205		for _, p := range paths {
206			edgePaths.WriteString(pathBlock(p.name, p.edgeIP, p.concIP+":"+strconv.Itoa(listenPort)))
207			concPaths.WriteString(pathBlock(p.name, p.concIP, ""))
208		}
209		primary := paths[0]
210		metricsBlock := "[metrics]\nlisten = \"" + metricsListen + "\"\n\n"
211	
212		dir := t.TempDir()
213		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"),
214			"role = \"edge\"\npsk = \""+psk+"\"\n\n"+
215				edgePaths.String()+metricsBlock+
216				"[wireguard]\nprivate_key = \""+edgePriv+"\"\n\n"+
217				"[[wireguard.peers]]\npublic_key = \""+concPub+"\"\n"+
218				"endpoint = \""+primary.concIP+":"+strconv.Itoa(listenPort)+"\"\n"+
219				"allowed_ips = [\""+concInner+"/32\"]\n\n"+
220				"[log]\nlevel = \"info\"\n")
221	
222		concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"),
223			"role = \"concentrator\"\npsk = \""+psk+"\"\n\n"+
224				concPaths.String()+
225				"[wireguard]\nprivate_key = \""+concPriv+"\"\nlisten_port = "+strconv.Itoa(listenPort)+"\n\n"+
226				"[[wireguard.peers]]\npublic_key = \""+edgePub+"\"\n"+
227				"allowed_ips = [\""+edgeInner+"/32\"]\n\n"+
228				"[log]\nlevel = \"info\"\n")
229	
230		conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg)
231		edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfg)
232	
233		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
234			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
235		}
236		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
237			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
238		}
239		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
240		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
241		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
242		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
243		return edge, conc
244	}
245	
246	// pathBlock renders one [[paths]] TOML block. destAddr is empty for the concentrator side
247	// (it has no dest_addr — it roams edges).
248	func pathBlock(name, sourceAddr, destAddr string) string {
249		b := "[[paths]]\nname = \"" + name + "\"\nsource_addr = \"" + sourceAddr + "\"\n"
250		if destAddr != "" {
251			b += "dest_addr = \"" + destAddr + "\"\n"
252		}
253		return b + "\n"
254	}

2659	// currently-active paths, in priority order — the read side the metrics exposition
2660	// (T23) scrapes. Concurrency: it acquires m.mu ONLY to copy out each path's name, its
2661	// two atomic byte counters, and its prober pointer, then RELEASES m.mu BEFORE calling
2662	// the (independently-synchronized) prober's Estimate()/State(). This mirrors
2663	// tickLivenessFromReceive's discipline — the lock is held for a bounded, syscall-free,
2664	// O(paths) copy and never across a prober call or a log write — so the scrape never
2665	// blocks an in-flight Send behind the prober's own mutex, and the lock-free send/
2666	// receive byte-counter Adds are undisturbed (they take no lock at all). The counters
2667	// are read as a point-in-time atomic Load: tx and rx of one path may reflect Adds a
2668	// nanosecond apart, which is immaterial to a cumulative-counter exposition.
2669	func (m *Multipath) PathSnapshots() []PathTraffic {
2670		type ref struct {
2671			name   string

2675		m.mu.Lock()
2676		refs := make([]ref, len(m.paths))

2682		out := make([]PathTraffic, len(refs))

343		// The engine has opened the bind (dev.Up → BindUpdate → Open), so the per-path
344		// sockets exist: start the probe cadence now. Close stops it before dev.Close.
345		// The interval is the SINGLE-SOURCE-OF-TRUTH telemetry default, which also arms
346		// the bind's receive-path liveness sweep throttle (D15).
347		stopProbes := mpBind.StartProbeLoop(telemetry.DefaultProbeInterval)
348		// Start the background deferred-path reconciler alongside probing (T55): a path the
349		// tolerant Open left DOWN (its source_addr not yet assignable — a 5G modem without a
350		// lease at boot) is retried and promoted to a live path as its address appears,
351		// WITHOUT a tunnel restart. Close stops it before dev.Close, like the probe loop.
352		stopReconcile := mpBind.StartReconcileLoop(bind.DefaultReconcileInterval)
353		// Start the edge-side hub-failover monitor (T57) AND the DNS re-resolution loop (T74): on hub
354		// loss (every path to the active concentrator DOWN) failover advances to the next ordered peer
355		// endpoint, repoints the bond's remote, and re-handshakes; re-resolution re-resolves opted-in
356		// hostname specs and installs/repoints on change. Both a no-op on the concentrator, a
357		// single-IP-literal edge, or a bind without the probe transport. Started AFTER dev.Up so the
358		// engine peer the re-handshake / endpoint-install look up exists (IpcSet added it above). Close
359		// stops both before dev.Close, like the probe/reconcile loops.
360		stopHubFailover, stopResolution := startFailoverAndResolution(cfg, mpBind, probers, dev, boot, clg)
361	
362		// The session monitor reads the engine's peer last-handshake state (I2). It backs BOTH
363		// the /metrics session snapshot (read at scrape time) and the 0->1 'session established'
364		// edge log (driven by its own poll loop). One instance is shared: it is stateless, so the
365		// concurrent scrape goroutine and the poll loop reading the same engine is safe.
366		sessMon := newSessionMonitor(dev, telemetry.SystemClock{})
367		// Start the WG-session monitor poll loop AFTER dev.Up so the engine peer it reads exists.
368		// Close stops it before dev.Close, like the probe/reconcile loops.
369		stopSession := startSessionMonitor(sessMon, sessionPollInterval, clg)
370	
371		t := &Tunnel{
372			dev: dev, tun: tunDev, name: name, bind: mpBind, cfg: cfg, log: clg,
373			stopProbes: stopProbes, stopReconcile: stopReconcile,
374			stopHubFailover: stopHubFailover, stopResolution: stopResolution,
375			stopSession: stopSession, amnezia: cfg.Amnezia,
376			// The Source reads live per-path counters/telemetry from the Bind and derives
377			// throughput from the byte-counter delta between scrapes (see metricsSource). The
378			// WG-session snapshot is read from the engine via sessMon. It is built unconditionally
379			// (cheap) so a reload that later turns [metrics].listen ON has a Source ready; the
380			// endpoint itself is started only when a listen is configured.
381			metricsSrc: newMetricsSource(mpBind, sessMon, telemetry.SystemClock{}),
382		}
383	
384		// Stand up the /metrics endpoint when configured. A non-loopback listen is refused
385		// by metrics.NewServer (fail fast) — surface it as an Up failure rather than booting
386		// a tunnel that silently exposes per-path operational data off-host.
387		t.reloadMu.Lock()
388		err = t.applyMetricsLocked(cfg.Metrics.Listen)
389		t.reloadMu.Unlock()
390		if err != nil {
391			// The tunnel is fully constructed and holds the amnezia guard, so transfer that
392			// ownership to it (ok=true) BEFORE tearing it down: t.Close releases the guard via
393			// releaseOnce, and suppressing the !ok defer avoids a double release. t.Close also
394			// stops the probe loop and closes the engine/TUN.
395			ok = true
396			t.Close()
397			return nil, fmt.Errorf("device: start metrics endpoint: %w", err)
398		}
399

1	package device
2	
3	import (
4		"sync"
5	
6		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/bind"
7		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/metrics"
8		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/telemetry"
9	)
10	
11	// trafficProvider is the read seam the metrics adapter consumes: the multipath Bind's
12	// per-PEER traffic+telemetry, FEC, and resequencer snapshot (T93/T94). *bind.Multipath
13	// satisfies it; a fake satisfies it in the adapter's unit test, so the mapping and rate
14	// derivation are testable without a running engine. PeerSnapshots generalizes the
15	// pre-T94 PathSnapshots/FECSnapshot (primary-peer-only) to every bound peer, so a
16	// single-peer edge/hub still maps 1:1 onto today's series (len(PeerSnapshots())==1) and
17	// a multi-peer concentrator's additional peers surface as additional entries.
18	type trafficProvider interface {
19		PeerSnapshots() []bind.PeerSnapshot
20	}
21	
22	// sampleKey identifies one (peer,path) pair in the throughput last-sample map (T94): a
23	// path name alone is only unique WITHIN one peer, not across peers (each peer's path
24	// set is independently configured/named in principle), so keying by path name alone
25	// would let two peers' samples for a same-named path clobber each other's rate
26	// derivation. peer is "" for the single-peer edge/hub and a concentrator's primary,
27	// exactly matching PeerSnapshot.Name — so a single-peer config's map degenerates to the
28	// pre-T94 keying (peer is always "", so the key varies only by path).
29	type sampleKey struct {
30		peer, path string
31	}
32	
33	// metricsSource adapts the multipath Bind onto metrics.Source: at every scrape it
34	// reads the Bind's cumulative per-(peer,path) byte counters + telemetry, and DERIVES
35	// the per-(peer,path) throughput as the rate of the (tx+rx) byte-counter delta since
36	// the previous scrape. Deriving the rate here (rather than in the Bind) keeps the
37	// Bind's hot path a pair of lock-free atomic Adds with no timekeeping, and confines the
38	// only stateful, scrape-cadence-dependent piece to this adapter. The first scrape of a
39	// (peer,path) reports zero throughput (no prior sample); a counter that appears to move
40	// backwards (only possible across a Close→Open counter reset) also yields zero for that
41	// interval rather than a spurious negative.
42	type metricsSource struct {
43		provider trafficProvider
44		// session yields the connection-scoped WG-session snapshot (I2), read from the
45		// engine at scrape time. It is separate from provider because the bind stays
46		// WG-unaware — the session signal is sourced by the device layer's engine seam.
47		session sessionSnapshotter
48		clock   telemetry.Clock
49	
50		mu   sync.Mutex
51		last map[sampleKey]byteSample
52	}
53	
54	// byteSample is the previous scrape's cumulative (tx+rx) total for one (peer,path) and
55	// the instant it was taken, so the next scrape can divide the delta by the elapsed time.
56	type byteSample struct {
57		total   uint64
58		atNanos int64
59	}
60	
61	// newMetricsSource builds a metrics.Source over the Bind (per-peer path traffic, FEC,
62	// and resequencer counters) and the engine session seam (WG-session snapshot). The
63	// clock is injected so the throughput derivation is deterministic under test.
64	func newMetricsSource(provider trafficProvider, session sessionSnapshotter, clock telemetry.Clock) *metricsSource {
65		return &metricsSource{provider: provider, session: session, clock: clock, last: make(map[sampleKey]byteSample)}
66	}
67	
68	// Paths implements metrics.Source. It is called on the scrape goroutine (the /metrics
69	// HTTP handler), which the prometheus registry may run concurrently, so the per-
70	// (peer,path) last-sample map is guarded. The underlying provider.PeerSnapshots() reads
71	// atomics and each prober's synchronized Estimate without holding the Bind's send lock
72	// across a prober call, so scraping never blocks Send.
73	func (s *metricsSource) Paths() []metrics.PathSnapshot {
74		peers := s.provider.PeerSnapshots()
75		nowNanos := s.clock.Now().UnixNano()
76	
77		total := 0
78		for _, peer := range peers {
79			total += len(peer.Paths)
80		}
81	
82		s.mu.Lock()
83		defer s.mu.Unlock()
84		out := make([]metrics.PathSnapshot, 0, total)
85		for _, peer := range peers {
86			for _, t := range peer.Paths {
87				key := sampleKey{peer: peer.Name, path: t.Name}
88				byteTotal := t.TxBytes + t.RxBytes
89				var tput float64
90				if prev, ok := s.last[key]; ok {
91					dtSeconds := float64(nowNanos-prev.atNanos) / 1e9
92					if dtSeconds > 0 && byteTotal >= prev.total {
93						tput = float64(byteTotal-prev.total) * 8 / dtSeconds
94					}
95				}
96				s.last[key] = byteSample{total: byteTotal, atNanos: nowNanos}
97				out = append(out, metrics.PathSnapshot{
98					Peer:                    peer.Name,
99					Name:                    t.Name,
100					TxBytes:                 t.TxBytes,
101					RxBytes:                 t.RxBytes,
102					ThroughputBitsPerSecond: tput,
103					Estimate:                t.Estimate,
104					State:                   t.State,
105				})
106			}
107		}
108		return out
109	}
110	
111	// FEC implements metrics.Source: it reads the Bind's per-peer connection-scoped FEC
112	// counters (T24, T94) verbatim at scrape time, one entry per bound peer. Unlike the
113	// per-path throughput, the FEC counters are cumulative and need no rate derivation or
114	// prior-sample state, so this is a direct pass-through of the Bind's lock-free snapshot.
115	func (s *metricsSource) FEC() []metrics.FECSnapshot {
116		peers := s.provider.PeerSnapshots()
117		out := make([]metrics.FECSnapshot, len(peers))
118		for i, peer := range peers {
119			f := peer.FEC
120			out[i] = metrics.FECSnapshot{
121				Peer:                 peer.Name,
122				DataPackets:          f.DataFrames,
123				RepairPackets:        f.ParityFrames,
124				RecoveredPackets:     f.Recovered,
125				UnrecoverablePackets: f.Unrecoverable,
126				DataBytes:            f.DataBytes,
127				RepairBytes:          f.ParityBytes,
128				ResidualLossRatio:    f.ResidualLoss,
129			}
130		}
131		return out
132	}
133	
134	// Reseq implements metrics.Source: it reads the Bind's per-peer resequencer counters
135	// (T94) verbatim at scrape time, one entry per bound peer — a direct pass-through of
136	// the resequencer's own cumulative Stats(), like FEC needing no rate derivation.
137	func (s *metricsSource) Reseq() []metrics.ReseqSnapshot {
138		peers := s.provider.PeerSnapshots()
139		out := make([]metrics.ReseqSnapshot, len(peers))
140		for i, peer := range peers {
141			out[i] = metrics.ReseqSnapshot{Peer: peer.Name, Stats: peer.Reseq}
142		}
143		return out
144	}
145	
146	// PeerNames implements metrics.Source: it returns the current bound-peer name set,
147	// queried once by metrics.NewCollector to fix the `peer` label's presence for the
148	// collector's whole life (T94) — see the metrics package doc for the back-compat rule.
149	func (s *metricsSource) PeerNames() []string {
150		peers := s.provider.PeerSnapshots()
151		names := make([]string, len(peers))
152		for i, peer := range peers {
153			names[i] = peer.Name
154		}
155		return names
156	}
157	
158	// Session implements metrics.Source: it reads the connection-scoped WG-session snapshot
159	// (I2) from the engine seam at scrape time. Like FEC it is a direct pass-through of a
160	// snapshot the underlying monitor computes on demand — no per-scrape state is kept here
161	// (the 0->1 edge log is driven by the separate session-monitor poll loop, not by scrapes).
162	func (s *metricsSource) Session() metrics.SessionSnapshot {
163		return s.session.SessionSnapshot()
164	}
